# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains benchmark-construction details, evaluation protocol details, and quantitative experimental results about experience reuse in repository-level programming agents.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: claim-support
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
SWE-ContextBench augments 300 base tasks with 99 related tasks derived from real issue and pull-request relationships.

## Source Excerpt

"Built on SWE-Bench Lite, SWE-ContextBench augments 300 base tasks with 99 related tasks derived from real dependency and reference relationships among GitHub issues and pull requests, forming task sequences with shared context."

## Note

This is a benchmark construction statement from the abstract.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §2.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The benchmark is designed to evaluate agents on accuracy, time efficiency, and cost efficiency.

## Source Excerpt

"We identify three key evaluation dimensions. 1) Accuracy. ... 2) Time efficiency. ... 3) Cost efficiency."

## Note

This states the benchmark's evaluation dimensions rather than an empirical outcome.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §2.3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The benchmark is built on SWE-Bench Lite, which contributes 300 real-world GitHub instances.

## Source Excerpt

"We build our benchmark on SWE-Bench Lite ... which consists of 300 instances drawn from real-world GitHub repositories."

## Note

This identifies the base dataset used to construct the benchmark.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: method
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §2.3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
For each SWE-Bench Lite instance, the authors run a base programming agent and store its full reasoning and interaction trajectory as retrievable past experience.

## Source Excerpt

"For each instance in SWE-Bench Lite, we first run a base programming agent to solve the task and record the full reasoning and interaction trajectory, including tool calls, file navigation, and intermediate reasoning steps. These trajectories are stored as past experience and serve as the experience pool..."

## Note

This describes how the reusable experience pool is created.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §2.3.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The first pass of related-task identification found 89 interdependent task instances, and each candidate was manually verified.

## Source Excerpt

"Across the dataset, we identify 89 such interdependent task instances... Each candidate task instance is manually verified to ensure that it corresponds to a meaningful software engineering task with a clear problem description and an identifiable resolution."

## Note

This count refers to the pre-recursive identification stage.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §2.3.4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Recursive context expansion added 10 more interdependent task instances beyond the initial 89.

## Source Excerpt

"This recursive process yields 10 additional interdependent task instances, forming new task sequences suitable for evaluating experience reuse."

## Note

This is a second-stage expansion result.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §2.3.5
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The final benchmark contains 300 experience tasks and 99 related tasks across 12 repositories.

## Source Excerpt

"The final benchmark consists of collections of related software engineering tasks... It includes 300 experience tasks, whose solution trajectories form a reusable experience pool, and 99 related tasks... Both experience and related tasks span 12 repositories..."

## Note

This is the final benchmark size after construction.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: method
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §2.3.6
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Each related task instance includes a pre-fix base_commit, a problem statement, and ground-truth code changes split into test_patch and solution_patch.

## Source Excerpt

"For each task, we include the repository state before the fix (base_commit), the problem statement, and the code changes that resolve the problem... The ground-truth solution is obtained from the pull request’s unified diff and is separated into two components: a test_patch and a solution_patch."

## Note

This describes the task-instance packaging used for evaluation.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: method
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §2.3.6
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Evaluation uses FAIL_TO_PASS tests to check whether the fix addresses the target issue and PASS_TO_PASS tests to check for regressions.

## Source Excerpt

"The FAIL_TO_PASS set consists of tests that were failing, erroring, or absent in results_before but pass in results_after, indicating that the fix successfully addresses the target issue. The PASS_TO_PASS set includes tests that pass both before and after patching, ensuring that the solution does not introduce regressions."

## Note

This is the paper's stated test-based validation rule.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: metric
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §2.3.6
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Across the 99 related tasks, the average task has 5.09 FAIL_TO_PASS tests and 128.32 PASS_TO_PASS tests.

## Source Excerpt

"On average, each of the 99 related tasks contains 5.09 FAIL_TO_PASS tests and 128.32 PASS_TO_PASS tests."

## Note

This is an average over the related-task set, not a per-repository constant.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: metric
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Summary experiences average 204.5 words, while full experience trajectories average 24,765 words.

## Source Excerpt

"The summary files contain an average of 204.5 words, whereas the full experience trajectories contain an average of 24,765 words, resulting in a substantially more compact representation of prior experience."

## Note

This quantifies the compactness difference between summary and full-trajectory context.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: result
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §3.3 Table 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Oracle Summary Reuse achieved the highest reported resolution rate at 34.34%, versus 26.26% for the No-Experience baseline.

## Source Excerpt

"Oracle Summary Reuse achieves the highest resolution rate at 34.34%, compared to 26.26% for the No-Experience baseline."

## Note

The source reports a performance difference between settings but does not directly establish a general causal mechanism beyond the tested benchmark configuration.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: result
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §3.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Oracle Experience Reuse improved the resolution rate only modestly to 27.27%, much less than Oracle Summary Reuse.

## Source Excerpt

"In contrast, Oracle Experience Reuse, which provides the full prior execution trajectory, yields only a modest improvement to 27.27%. This suggests that concise summaries are more effective than raw execution traces when the relevant experience is known."

## Note

This introduces tension against a simple assumption that more raw prior context is always better; the comparison is within this benchmark and these retrieval settings.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: result
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §3.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Free Summary Reuse underperformed the baseline at 22.22% resolved and had the most patch application failures at 7 instances.

## Source Excerpt

"Free Summary Reuse is the only setting that underperforms the baseline..." Table 3 reports "Resolved 22.22%" for Free Summary Reuse versus "26.26%" for No-Experience, and "Patch N/A 7" for Free Summary Reuse, the highest among methods.

## Note

This is a negative result showing that summary reuse without reliable selection can be harmful in this setup.
```

### file: evidence-15.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-036
evidence_id: evidence-15
kind: result
citation: Zhu et al., 2026, §§3.4-3.5
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08316
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Oracle Summary Reuse had the lowest average runtime at 356.95 seconds per task and the lowest average cost at $0.77 per instance.

## Source Excerpt

"Among the five approaches, Oracle Summary Reuse achieves the lowest average running time, at 356.95 seconds per task." "Oracle Summary Reuse achieves the lowest average cost at $0.77 per instance..."

## Note

The source ties these efficiency gains to the oracle-guided summary setting tested here; it does not show that all summary-based reuse will reduce runtime or cost in other settings.
---
